title: Presigned Browser Uploads, POST Policies, and CORS · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent team receives different tenants, file sizes, media types, regions, compliance duties, traffic, failures, and cost limits and transfers presigned browser uploads, post policies, and cors into a new least-privilege direct-upload session contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of least-privilege direct-upload session contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.
Presign PUT with exact region, expiry, key, checksum, content type, encryption, and required header parity.
Use a POST policy when browser-enforced key, size, metadata, and field conditions are required and validate the completed object independently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Upload Session Intent
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 2719 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to finalize a direct upload against intent while separating CORS from IAM authorization; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Upload Session Intent.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Upload Session Intent, keep Upload Session Intent connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 2719 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to finalize a direct upload against intent while separating CORS from IAM authorization; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.

A presigned URL can safely authorize any file that the browser chooses later. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.
For commit to the mission model, run one convenient Upload Session Intent happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Audit the initial evidence packet · Lab Direct Upload Finalization
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 4247 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to finalize a direct upload against intent while separating CORS from IAM authorization; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Finalize by matching intent to HEAD or event evidence, checksum, version, size, owner, scan result, and idempotent state transition.

Inspect the Direct Upload Finalization trace for this lab case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
